qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of including `wandb offline` in a script when using Weights & Biases?
qa_pairs.answer: Including `wandb offline` in a script specifies that Weights & Biases should operate in offline mode. In this mode, `wandb` will write all metrics, logs, and artifacts to the local disk and will not attempt to sync anything to the Weights & Biases service on the internet during the job.

qa_pairs.question: How does the `wandb-test.py` script demonstrate logging metrics with Weights & Biases?
qa_pairs.answer: The `wandb-test.py` script demonstrates logging metrics by first initializing Weights & Biases with `wandb.init(project="wandb-pytorch-test", settings=wandb.Settings(start_method="fork"))`. It then logs a metric named 'my_metric' within a loop using `wandb.log({'my_metric': my_metric})` for a range of 10 iterations.

qa_pairs.question: After a training run in offline mode, how can users upload their collected metrics to the Weights & Biases server?
qa_pairs.answer: After a training run in offline mode, a new folder named `./wandb/offline-run*` will be created. Users can send the metrics to the Weights & Biases server by running the command `wandb sync ./wandb/offline-run*`. Using `*` will sync all runs.
